name: troposphere (Python CloudFormation Templates)
pricing_notes: Open source (New BSD); cloudtools / Mark Peek

auth.notes: Template generation only; deployment via AWS CloudFormation CLI/SDK

performance.notes: Template generation is fast; deployment via CloudFormation can take minutes

agent_readiness.notes: Python library for generating CloudFormation templates. from troposphere import Template, Output, Ref. from troposphere.ec2 import Instance, SecurityGroup. t = Template(). sg = t.add_resource(SecurityGroup('WebSG', GroupDescription='Web SG', SecurityGroupIngress=[{'IpProtocol':'tcp','FromPort':80,'ToPort':80,'CidrIp':'0.0.0.0/0'}])). instance = t.add_resource(Instance('WebServer', ImageId='ami-12345', InstanceType='t3.micro', SecurityGroups=[Ref(sg)])). t.add_output(Output('PublicIP', Value=GetAtt('WebServer', 'PublicIp'))). print(t.to_json()). t.to_yaml(). Supports all CloudFormation resources. Validate() for basic checks. Custom resources. Condition/Parameter support. Used to generate JSON/YAML that deploys via CloudFormation.

gotchas: Generates CloudFormation JSON/YAML — still subject to CloudFormation's own quirks
No type annotations — limited IDE support
AWS CDK and Pulumi offer better abstraction for most new projects
Validation is basic — many errors only surface during actual deployment
Learning both troposphere and CloudFormation concepts required
best_when: Existing CloudFormation shops wanting programmatic template generation
When staying in CloudFormation ecosystem but want Python ergonomics
Generating parameterized CloudFormation from Python data
avoid_when: New IaC projects — use AWS CDK or Pulumi instead
When cross-cloud IaC is needed (use Pulumi or Terraform)
When CloudFormation itself is not already the deployment mechanism
summary: troposphere generates AWS CloudFormation templates in Python. t = Template(); t.add_resource(Instance(...)); t.add_output(Output(...)); t.to_json(). Supports all CloudFormation resource types. BSD by cloudtools. Best for existing CloudFormation shops wanting Python ergonomics. No type annotations. AWS CDK is the modern alternative for new projects. Validation limited — errors surface at deploy time.
